models.description: Restricted master problem (a linear program solved by CPLEX) of the branch-price-and-cut (BPC) algorithm for the multigraph Electric Vehicle Routing and Overnight Charging Scheduling Problem with Time Windows (mE-VRSPTW). A set-partitioning formulation over the set of feasible vehicle routes (columns): minimize total routing cost subject to visiting each customer exactly once and respecting the number of chargers available at the depot in each charging period. A (weak) rounded-capacity inequality on the number of vehicles is added, and Subset-Row Cuts (Jepsen et al. 2008) plus branching constraints are added during the BPC search. Columns (routes) are generated by a separate ng-SPPRC labeling pricing algorithm (see notes). From Yamin, Desaulniers & Mendoza (INFORMS J. Computing, 2024).

sets_indices.name: C
sets_indices.meaning: set of customers i in {1,...,C}; each must be visited exactly once

sets_indices.name: P
sets_indices.meaning: set of charging periods (discrete timesteps) t in {1,...,last_charging_period}

sets_indices.name: Omega
sets_indices.meaning: set of feasible routes/columns r (one master variable per generated route); generated dynamically by the pricing problem

sets_indices.name: S
sets_indices.meaning: set of separated Subset-Row Cut customer triplets (each a 3-element subset of C)

parameters.name: c_r
parameters.meaning: cost of route (column) r, entered as the objective coefficient when the column is added

parameters.name: a_{ir}
parameters.meaning: number of times route r visits customer i (coefficient in the partitioning row of customer i)

parameters.name: b_{tr}
parameters.meaning: 1 if route r occupies a charger during period t, i.e. t in [initialChargingTime, initialChargingTime+chargingTime-1]; 0 otherwise

parameters.name: B
parameters.meaning: number of chargers available at the depot (RHS of charger-capacity constraints)

parameters.name: Kmin
parameters.meaning: minimum number of vehicles = ceil(total customer load / vehicle load capacity Q); RHS of the rounded-capacity inequality

parameters.name: Q
parameters.meaning: vehicle load capacity (used to compute Kmin)

parameters.name: q_i
parameters.meaning: load (demand) of customer i (summed into totalLoad)

parameters.name: alpha_{rS}
parameters.meaning: coefficient of route r in Subset-Row Cut S = floor(0.5 * number of visits route r makes to the triplet S)

decision_variables.name: x_r
decision_variables.meaning: use of route (column) r in the master LP relaxation; integrality is enforced by branching in the surrounding branch-and-price tree, so the master itself is an LP with x_r >= 0

objective.expression_plain: minimize total cost of selected routes; each column registers with the objective via its cost (cplex.addMinimize at build time, cplex.column(obj, column.cost) when a column is added)

constraints.name: visit_customer_partition
constraints.expression_plain: each customer i must be visited exactly once across the selected routes (set-partitioning equality constraints; one IloRange per customer)

constraints.name: charger_capacity_per_period
constraints.expression_plain: in each charging period t, the number of vehicles occupying a charger may not exceed the number of available chargers B

constraints.name: rounded_capacity_inequality
constraints.expression_plain: (weak) rounded-capacity inequality: at least Kmin = ceil(total load / vehicle capacity) routes (vehicles) must be used. Real (non-artificial) columns enter this row with coefficient 1; the artificial column enters with coefficient Kmin

constraints.name: subset_row_cut
constraints.indexed_over: S in S (customer triplet, |S|=3); added during the cut-generation loop only when violated
constraints.expression_plain: Subset-Row Cut over a customer triplet S (Jepsen et al. 2008): the rounded number of visits to S across selected routes is at most 1. Added dynamically as <= 1 inequalities during BPC

constraints.name: branching_number_of_vehicles
constraints.indexed_over: branching decisions in the BPC tree (one per active NumberVehiclesInequalities)
constraints.expression_plain: branching constraint on the total number of vehicles used: sum of route variables <= or >= an integer coefficient kappa. Added/removed as the branch-and-price tree branches on the number of vehicles

constraints.name: branching_charging_time
constraints.indexed_over: branching decisions in the BPC tree (one per active ChargingTimeInequality)
constraints.expression_plain: branching constraint on charging start/end times: routes whose charging start (or end) timestep equals the branch timestep are summed and bounded above/below by an integer coefficient. Added/removed as the BPC tree branches on initial/end charging times

instances.name: {D}{T}{m}-{n}.xml
instances.size: 112 instances (e.g. R101-25.xml ~414 KB). Solomon-derived: D in {R,C,RC} customer distribution, T in {1,2} time-window tightness, m two-digit instance number, n in {25,50} customers. Each file carries network (nodes/links with travel_cost, travel_time, energy_consumption), fleet (chargers, energy_capacity, piecewise-linear inverse recharging function) and requests. Format documented in data/README.txt

models.notes: The master is built as a pure LP in CPLEX: obj = cplex.addMinimize(); customer rows = cplex.addEq(...,1); charger rows = cplex.addLe(...,B); rounded-capacity row = cplex.addGe(...,Kmin); all master variables are continuous (numVar lower bound 0, upper bound MAX). Integrality of route selection is enforced by branching in the surrounding jORLib branch-and-price tree (src/branchAndPrice/), not by integer variables in this LP. The rounded-capacity inequality, Subset-Row Cuts, and the two families of branching constraints are added/removed dynamically during the BPC search rather than being in the initial model. Costs and the objective are scaled by 10 (integer travel distances; EVRPTWSolver.getScaledObjective divides by 10). The PRICING SUBPROBLEM is NOT formulated as an (MI)LP: it is an elementary/ng-path shortest-path problem with resource constraints (ng-SPPRC over a directed weighted multigraph with battery/energy, time-window and charging-schedule resources) solved by a dynamic-programming LABELING algorithm (src/columnGeneration/ExactLabelingMultigraphPricingProblemSolver.java and the three Heuristic*Labeling*PricingProblemSolver classes). Those solvers import no CPLEX/Ilo classes. Per the extraction rules, no LP was invented for the pricing problem; it is recorded here as an algorithm only. Branching-rule, fix/remove-arc and additional inequality classes live in src/branchAndPrice/.

extraction_notes: Master LP transcribed directly from CPLEX Concert calls in src/columnGeneration/Master.java (buildModel lines 57-93 create the rows; addColumn lines 186-249 set the column coefficients; addCut lines 253-291 add SRC rows; addBranchingOnVehichlesInequality/addChargingTimeInequality lines 456-483 add branching rows). No written mathematical formulation ships in the repository (README.md describes only data format, dependencies and how to run; the full model lives in the paper, https://doi.org/10.1287/ijoc.2023.0404, which was NOT consulted). The math symbols here (x_r, a_ir, b_tr, B, Kmin, alpha_rS) are the extractor's own mapping of the Java identifiers, hence not verbatim paper notation; code identifiers are preserved in source_symbol. paper_reference DOI 10.1287/ijoc.2023.0404 is taken verbatim from README.md (not inferred). The repo is a single distinct optimization model (the master set-partitioning LP); the pricing problem is an algorithm, not an LP, and is documented in notes rather than added as a second models[] entry. License: MIT (LICENSE.txt, Copyright 2024 Daniel Yamin), copied verbatim to INFORMSJoC__2023.0404.LICENSE.
